title: Hedging flip: suppress flip-open when partial close under-delivers (sub-min residual)
source: Adversarial code review 2026-05-29 (risk-officer + general-purpose reviewers on armed execution layer)
why: On a RETAIL_HEDGING flip, if the close under-delivers by <=1 volume_min the flip-open still fires, leaving a tiny opposite-side residual ticket. Bounded + self-heals via broker-truth reconcile, but grows ticket count (related B0092).
scope: risk-officer FINDING2: in _reduce_or_flip_hedging, _close_lots_by_ticket may break on a sub-min residual (snapped<volume_min); achieved net then falls short of target, yet flip_open fires unconditionally. FIX: verify achieved net vs target after the close loop; on a flip do NOT fire flip_open if the close under-delivered; alarm when a needed reduction is blocked by the broker minimum.

history.reason: Logged from adversarial review of armed execution layer

fix(execution): adversarial review caught 2 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH bugs in armed layer

Resolved: flip-compounding is near-unreachable on a clean book (a flip close targets abs(net), always step-aligned -> closes in full; only partial REDUCES leave a bounded <1 volume_min residual that self-heals). _close_lots_by_ticket now returns the undelivered remainder; caller alarms on under-delivery and DEFENSIVELY suppresses flip-open on material under-delivery. TDD test added.
fix(execution): resolve B0093/B0094/B0095 review findings; B0090 -> done
